text: Trump: The Iranian people will fight back as soon as they get weapons. If they had weapons, Iran would give up in two seconds.

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Trump on the rescue operation to save the two crew members of the F-15 downed over Iran last week. "And what about the rescue that took place yesterday? What about that? That's something that you rarely see. You know, they were giving me a briefing about that. And they said, normally, when you're in very hostile territory, and I don't think it gets much more hostile than Iran, they're capable fighters. They're very tough people. And there are others like that. You don't mind when the enemy is weak, but that enemy is strong. Not so strong like they were about a month ago. I can tell you, in fact, right now, they're not too strong at all, in my opinion, but we're soon gonna find out, aren't we?" @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei in a interview with Reuters warned that if U.S. forces attack Iranian power plants and electricity grids, Iran will retaliate by striking similar U.S. infrastructure in the region. He said Iran’s response would be defensive and proportionate, not a choice but a necessary measure. Baqaei also accused the U.S. of lying about an Iranian nuclear threat and claimed that a deliberate strike on a primary school in Minab and a sports hall in Lamerd killed more than 200 children in less than an hour. He called on the American people to hold their government accountable for what he described as war crimes committed in their name. @wfwitness

Earlier, the same South Pars complex in Assaluyeh was hit on March 18, targeting a gas processing plant. Israeli Occupation Minister Israel Katz stated that both facilities, which he claims account for around 85% of Iran’s oil and gas chemical exports, have been disabled and are no longer operational. He added that the IOF has been ordered to continue strikes on Iran’s national infrastructure. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
